problem.title: Reverse Linked List II
problem.description: Given the head of a singly linked list and two integers left and right where left <= right, reverse the nodes of the list from position left to position right, and return the reversed list. Do it in one pass.
assumptions: Must be done in one pass.

example.explanation: Reverse positions 2-4: [2,3,4] → [4,3,2]. Use a dummy node before head. Walk to position left-1, then iteratively insert the next node after the sublist start (front-insertion / head-insertion reversal).

solutions.title: Solution 1 – One-Pass Front Insertion [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Use a dummy node pointing to head. Walk to the node just before position left (call it prev). Then repeat (right - left) times: take the node immediately after prev.next, remove it from its current position, and insert it right after prev. This reverses the sublist in-place in one pass with O(1) extra space.
